# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Test

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      ENVIRONMENT: 'development'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Cache node_modules
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        id: cache-node-modules
        with:
          path: ~/.npm-packages/node_modules
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - name: Install dependencies (Node.js)
        run: npm ci

      - name: Cache pip packages
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        id: cache-pip-packages
        with:
          path: ~/.python_packages/lib/python3.9/site-packages
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Install dependencies (Python)
        run: pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

      - name: Cache configuration files
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        id: cache-config-files
        with:
          path: ~/.config
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-config-${{ hashFiles('application.yml') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-config-

      - name: Build and test application
        run: make build test
